perturbation.original_text: "Product" means the commodities commonly associated as specialty petroleum products, specifically condensate, natural gasoline, and diluent. Product may also include liquefied petroleum gas commodities such as butane. Product, as designed in this Agreement, does not refer to finished gasoline and diesel products.
perturbation.changed_text: "Product" means any liquid commodity transported via pipeline, including, but not limited to, condensate, natural gasoline, diluent, butane, finished gasoline, and diesel products. The inclusion of finished gasoline and diesel is at the sole discretion of MPL.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Product' explicitly excludes finished gasoline and diesel products. The modified definition includes these products and introduces the discretion for MPL to include or exclude them. This creates a contradiction if other sections of the agreement rely on the original, narrower definition of 'Product', especially concerning volume commitments and transportation charges, since now MPL has the option to move any commodity including gasoline and diesel; potentially disrupting the balance of the agreement. The change is directly contradicting the volume committment by the shipper on the Capacity Request Form in Exhibit C
perturbation.contradicted_text: Include copy of Shipper's submitted Capacity Request Form (CFR) indicating desired routes and destinations. perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Product' explicitly excludes finished gasoline and diesel, while the modified definition includes them at MPL's discretion. The Capacity Request Forms in the contradicted section explicitly list 'Product Type' as [Condensate, Natural Gasoline, Diluent or Butane], excluding finished gasoline and diesel. The change creates a direct conflict as it allows for transportation of products not covered by the volume commitments specified in the Capacity Request Forms, thus disrupting the balance of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Shipper guarantees that during each Contract Year, Shipper will meet its Quarterly Volume Commitment or, in the event it fails to do so, shall remit to MPL the Quarterly Deficiency Payment pursuant to Section 3.5. All volumes moved by Shipper on the Pipeline will be subject to the applicable Tariff Rates and proration policies, as may be amended from time to time in accordance with FERC methodologies and as provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Shipper shall use reasonable efforts to meet its Quarterly Volume Commitment during each Contract Year, but failure to do so will not result in any deficiency payment. All volumes moved by Shipper on the Pipeline will be subject to the applicable Tariff Rates and proration policies, as may be amended from time to time in accordance with FERC methodologies and as provided herein. The definition of Quarterly Volume Committment is now considered as an estimate and not a binding obligation.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that the Shipper 'guarantees' to meet its Quarterly Volume Commitment, and failure to do so results in a 'Quarterly Deficiency Payment.' The modification replaces 'guarantees' with 'shall use reasonable efforts' and removes the consequence of failing to meet the commitment, stating there will be no deficiency payment. This directly contradicts the definition and the consequences clearly established later in section 3.5, which details how the Quarterly Deficiency Payment is calculated and applied. By changing the nature of the Quarterly Volume Commitment from a guarantee to an estimated target, the effectiveness of section 3.5 has been voided.
perturbation.location: 3. Volume Commitment and Quarterly Deficiency Charges
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions of Section 5 of this Agreement, if the volume of each Product shipped by Shipper on the Pipeline during the Quarter is less than the applicable Quarterly Volume Commitment for that Product then, in addition to paying any amounts incurred by Shipper pursuant to Section 3.4 with respect to Shipper Deliveries for such Quarter, Shipper shall also pay MPL a deficiency payment (the "Quarterly Deficiency Payment"); equal to the product of: (a) the difference between the applicable Quarterly Volume Commitment for that Product for such Quarter and the volume of Shipper Deliveries of that Product on the Pipeline for such Quarter (the "Deficiency Volume"); and (b) the applicable Tariff Rate for that Product for such Quarter. Each Product to which the Shipper is obligated to move as part of its committed volumes will be added together for one total Quarterly Deficiency Payment. Shipper's transportation of commodities outside this Agreement's definition of Product will not satisfy the obligation to move committed Product volume. All Product barrels moved by Shipper in excess of its Quarterly Volume Commitment will be attributed to Shipper's movements on the Pipeline as a Regular Shipper, subject to the applicable Rules & Regulations of the Tariff.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3.1 replaces a guarantee with 'reasonable efforts' and explicitly states no deficiency payment will occur. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the calculation and application of the 'Quarterly Deficiency Payment' when the Shipper fails to meet the Quarterly Volume Commitment. By changing the obligation to a non-binding estimate, the section outlining the deficiency payment becomes unenforceable and directly contradicts the modified section 3.1.

perturbation.original_text: 17.1 Financial Information. Shipper shall provide to MPL, at any time: (i) upon MPL's request, information ("Financial Information") that will allow MPL to assess (or reassess) and establish creditworthiness and Shipper's capacity to perform any financial obligations that could arise from the transportation of Shipper's Crude Petroleum on the Pipeline; and (ii) upon MPL's good faith determination (which shall be no less than industry standards) of non-creditworthiness, MPL may request, financial assurance in respect of transportation or other services ("Financial Assurances"). Financial Assurances shall be limited to a guarantee from the parent company of Shipper in a form and substance acceptable to MPL and sufficient in amount to cover 6-months of Shipper's obligations to MPL so long as the guarantor has sufficient creditworthiness as set forth in this Section; and if not, MPL may then request an irrevocable standby letter of credit in a form and from an issuer acceptable to MPL, and in an amount no greater than 6-months of tolls based on the Quarterly Volume Commitment, plus all applicable taxes.
perturbation.changed_text: 17.1 Financial Information. Shipper shall provide to MPL, at any time: (i) upon MPL's request, information ("Financial Information") that will allow MPL to assess (or reassess) and establish creditworthiness and Shipper's capacity to perform any financial obligations that could arise from the transportation of Shipper's Crude Petroleum on the Pipeline; and (ii) MPL may request, financial assurance in respect of transportation or other services ("Financial Assurances"), even without a determination of non-creditworthiness. Financial Assurances shall be an irrevocable standby letter of credit in a form and from an issuer acceptable to MPL, and in an amount equal to 12-months of tolls based on MPL's estimation of Shipper's potential throughput, plus all applicable taxes. The need for any financial asssurances is at the sole discretiion of the MPL.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says that MPL can request the Financial Assurances, if MPL determined the Shipper to be with a non-creditworthiness, but that determintation should be no less than the industry standards. The changed texts removes that condition and it may request the Financial Assurances regardless of that previous condition and industry standards. Also, the text says the Financial Assurances will be the letter of credit, and the ammount has changed from 6-months to 12-months. This will contradict what is being expressed in Section 17.2, because the Tariff has not yet been created, and with this change, will allow MPL to charge whatever amount it is convenient to them, and it won't be necessary to follow through the Tariff.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As of the Project's in-service date, Shipper shall comply with the credit requirements and provide the financial assurances required in the Tariff so long as the Tariff is consistent with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 17.1 removes the requirement that MPL's determination of non-creditworthiness meet industry standards, and alters the conditions under which Financial Assurances can be requested. It also increases the amount to be covered by the letter of credit from 6 months to 12 months based on MPL's estimation and dictates that the financial assurance *will be* a letter of credit (vs possibly a guarantee). This directly contradicts the provision in the 'Contradicted Text' (section stating 'Shipper shall comply with the credit requirements and provide the financial assurances required in the Tariff so long as the Tariff is consistent with the terms of this Agreement') because the Tariff is not yet created, and the changed language gives MPL significantly more discretion, potentially allowing requirements inconsistent with the agreement.
